description_long: This is a medium-sized, bulbous terracotta vase with a wide, flared neck and a narrow base. The body is adorned with a horizontal band of geometric patterns, including lines and cross motifs, rendered in a contrasting lighter color. Two small, rounded handles are positioned symmetrically on either side of the vase, just below the patterned band. The surface has a matte, slightly rough finish, typical of hand-crafted pottery. The coloration is predominantly a warm reddish-brown, with decorative accents in beige and dark brown. The vase appears well-preserved, with no visible cracks or major chips.
description: A reddish-brown terracotta vase with a flared neck, geometric banding, and two small side handles. Matte finish with decorative patterns and a sturdy, rounded form.
description_view_0: The vase is seen from an angle highlighting one handle and the geometric band, with the flared neck and rounded body clearly visible.
description_view_1: This view shows the vase's front, displaying both handles and the full decorative band with central motifs.
description_view_2: The opposite side from view 0, showing the other handle and the continuation of the geometric pattern.
description_view_3: The back of the vase, with both handles visible but minimal decoration, emphasizing the plain reddish-brown surface.
